texanshootR 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Getting started with texanshootR
  • Changelog

Getting started with texanshootR

Source: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd
getting-started.Rmd

texanshootR provides a structured, terminal-first interface for exploratory linear-model search. The main entry point is shoot().

A first run 

library(texanshootR)

run <- shoot(mtcars, theatrical = FALSE, budget = 5)
print(run)
summary(run)

The returned tx_run object stores the highlighted specification, the search summary, and any life events that fired during the run.

The career

The package keeps a persistent career across runs in tools::R_user_dir("texanshootR", "data"). Use career() to see your current state:

career()

The first time you save, you’ll be prompted to confirm the path. Set options(texanshootR.save_enabled = FALSE) to opt out entirely.

Output generators

Each output function takes a tx_run and writes to tempdir() by default. Override with output_dir or options(texanshootR.output_dir = ...):

graphical_abstract(run)
manuscript(run)
preprint(run)
reviewer_response(run)
funding(run)
presentation(run)

Output functions return the file path invisibly and print a short status line.

Achievements and cosmetics 

achievements()
wardrobe()

Hidden achievements appear as ??? until unlocked. New cosmetics auto-equip when their unlocking achievement fires; override the selection with wardrobe(slot, id).

Resetting

If you want to start over:

reset_career(force = TRUE)
reset_all(force = TRUE)

On this page

Developed by Gilles Colling.
